summary: When I arrived in Minnesota in August 2021, I started my exchange student life at the University of Minnesota. It was a big change for me, especially adapting to the culture of small talk, which is common in the US but not in Korea or Japan. The university gave me a chance to experience a new educational environment after the pandemic disrupted my studies in Japan. My time at UMN helped broaden my perspective and made my college experience more meaningful.

Japan became a significant part of my life when I first visited Tokyo at age 16 and was captivated by its beautiful night views and peaceful atmosphere. I decided to attend a Japanese university to fulfill my dream of living in a different culture. I moved to Japan two years after my first visit and experienced many challenges during my freshman year, including language barriers and cultural adjustments. The pandemic forced me to return to Korea and take online classes, but Japan remains a place where I grew a lot personally and academically.

I was born and raised in Korea, specifically in Seongnam near Seoul, where I lived for 19 years before moving abroad. Korea is my home, and even though I have lived in Japan and the US, my roots are deeply connected there. During the pandemic, I returned to Korea to take online classes from my Japanese university. Now, as I prepare to go back, I feel grateful for the experiences I've had abroad but am happy to return to my home country.

Seongnam is the city where I was born and lived for 19 years before moving abroad. It is a planned city near Seoul with very organized buildings and roads, and every neighborhood looked quite similar. I often felt bored living in such a uniform environment, which fueled my desire to explore other cultures and places. Growing up in Seongnam shaped my early life but also motivated me to seek adventure elsewhere.

Seoul is the major city close to where I grew up in Seongnam. Although I lived in Seongnam, Seoul was always nearby and part of my environment. My upbringing near Seoul gave me a sense of urban life in Korea, but I wanted to experience something different, which led me to travel to Tokyo and eventually study abroad. Seoul represents the starting point of my journey before I ventured out into the world.

Tokyo was the city that first inspired my dream to live abroad when I visited at age 16. I was fascinated by its beautiful night views and the calm atmosphere, which was very different from my hometown. Two years later, I moved to Tokyo to attend university, marking the beginning of my life in Japan. Living in Tokyo was challenging at first, especially with language and cultural differences, but it became a place where I grew and learned a lot.
